brief: Out of 1,271 parcels scanned and 920 pitching out as viable, opportunities are heavily concentrated in Brooklyn R7A/R7-2 city-owned lots utilizing a 485-x tax exemption paired with Section 8 VPS rents. These Brooklyn market-tier parcels generate strong Cash-on-Cash returns up to 13.7% with nearly $1M in land valuation headroom per site. In contrast, Bronx program-tier deals relying on the ELLA / 4% LIHTC stack show negative land headroom against comp estimates ($324/PSF), making them reliant on developer fees and competitive subsidy awards.

targets.play: Develop a 12-unit residential project in a Brooklyn R7A zone leveraging 485-x tax exemption with Section 8 VPS rents for a 13.7% Cash-on-Cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $591,738 offers $996,310 in headroom against the $1,588,048 walk-away price.
targets.risk: Navigating the HPD/EDC disposition process for city-owned land and verifying site conditions and zoning.

targets.play: Execute a 10-unit residential development in a Brooklyn R7-2 zone utilizing 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents to achieve a 12.7% Cash-on-Cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $569,141 provides $956,661 in headroom below the $1,525,802 walk-away limit.
targets.risk: City-owned HPD disposition RFP requirements and zoning verification.

targets.play: Build 14 units in a Brooklyn R7A zone using 485-x plus Section 8 VPS rents targeting a 12.4% Cash-on-Cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $690,612 creates $1,070,155 in headroom relative to the $1,760,767 walk-away land cost.
targets.risk: HPD/EDC disposition process and site condition assessment.

targets.play: Deliver 14 units in Queens under an R6A zoning designation using 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents for an 11.2% Cash-on-Cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $622,014 yields $909,591 in headroom against the $1,531,605 walk-away limit.
targets.risk: Site condition due diligence and HPD disposition process for city-owned property.

process_note: Because these screening estimates are subject to diligence and these city-owned sites require HPD/EDC disposition via public RFPs rather than direct purchase, our next step is to run full zoning verification and site condition checks while preparing for upcoming city RFPs.
disclaimer: AI interpretation of the engine's real screening output. Estimates pending diligence — not investment advice.
